justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus an extra BR-5 for manager authorization). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day notice (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities formally surfaced (partial month accrual, retroactive window anchor, balance deduction timing, cancellation, forfeiture legality). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria well exceed the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 68 is appropriately calibrated given the ambiguities identified.

observations.text: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classifications — no misclassifications
Clear distinction between structural invariants (balance integrity, contract boundary) and policy rules (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice period)
12 acceptance criteria with well-constructed Given/When/Then format, covering accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, and more
5 formal escalations surfacing ambiguities including the two specifically expected: contract-end mid-period and 5-day window calendar vs working days
Rich edge cases section covering concurrent requests, partial month accrual, and contract date changes
Added BR-5 (manager authorization invariant) beyond the expected 7 rules — reasonable domain insight
Comprehensive business impact analysis covering financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with stakeholder mapping

Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than distinguishing 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management' as separate bounded contexts per the rubric — though the rules themselves correctly span both concerns
